purpose: Interface-level rate-limit and backoff notes for the hidden champion screener. Keep this file close to live observations so future batch runs do not rediscover the same limits.

notes: This screener is low-frequency. Prefer waiting over parallelizing.
For whole-universe jobs, run market/valuation first, then slower text/LLM sources only on shortlisted candidates.
Observed limits are from local runs and may vary by OpenD version, account permission, market, or vendor policy.
Used for market-cap seeding.
For 500M-1B US market cap, returned 584 raw matches and 502 common-stock tickers after local filtering.
Used for price, market cap, P/E, P/B, volume, turnover, and 52-week range.
502-symbol 500M-1B run completed in 100-symbol batches without hitting this interface limit.

observed_error_patterns: Get Stock's Sector interface does not support ETFs type.
notes: Some symbols returned by stock_filter still cause sector API errors. Do not fail the whole batch.
If unsupported, keep company sector as Unclassified and continue.

get_capital_distribution.observed_limit: Not separately hit in current runs
notes: Used for current super/big/mid/small buy and sell amounts.
Some responses may have empty update_time; treat empty date strings as null before PostgreSQL writes.

observed_error_patterns: Get Capital Flow request failed due to high frequency. Maximum 30 times per 30 seconds.
notes: Initial 500M-1B Futu-only run called this too quickly: 150/502 symbols got attention_flow and 352 symbols hit the 30-per-30s limit.
Do not parallelize this interface.
For large universes, keep 100-symbol batches but allow the per-symbol attention loop to sleep about 1.05 seconds.

request_history_kline.observed_limit: Historical candlestick quota can be exhausted; quota is released on a rolling basis and may take up to 30 days to fully release.
observed_error_patterns: Quota is released on a rolling basis
notes: Used for 5/20/60-day returns and 60-day price range position.
Rate-limit pacing is shared with the attention_flow composite rather than separately enforced.
If this quota is exhausted, still save capital_distribution and capital_flow; mark recent return metrics as missing instead of failing the whole attention_flow item.
This composite is the bottleneck for large Futu-only scans.
It should be optional or staged after market-cap/valuation seeding.
In the 500M-1B補跑, runs 31-32 hit historical candlestick quota, not capital-flow frequency; this should degrade to missing recent-return metrics.
SEC archive document downloads can occasionally EOF mid TLS handshake. Retry before failing the run.
Keep SEC work downstream of Futu shortlists.
Use SEC_USER_AGENT.
Current pipeline relies on cache and per-ticker delay; do not run broad SEC scans over hundreds of names unless intentionally scheduled.
Optional slow path. Only run after the candidate list is already small.
Keep default off. Prefer Futu OpenD for market/valuation fields.
Use yFinance only as a slow fallback for ownership/fundamental fields that are not available from Futu or SEC.
Default off. Used for SEC snippet summaries and company summary panel.
If 429 occurs, do not fail the whole run; fall back to heuristic summary where possible.
No external rate limit. Keep writes single-threaded for pipeline simplicity.
Do not write empty strings into timestamptz fields; normalize empty dates to null.

known_run_notes.result: 502 symbols completed for snapshot/valuation/sector. attention_flow completed for 150 symbols before get_capital_flow rate-limit errors dominated.
known_run_notes.lesson: Use get_capital_flow <= 30 calls per 30 seconds; single-thread and per-symbol delay around 1.05 seconds.

known_run_notes.result: Failed on owner_plate batch due to unsupported ETF-like symbol.
known_run_notes.lesson: owner_plate batch errors must fall back to one-by-one and skip unsupported symbols.
